subject: a newborn foal, wobbly legs and oversized head
details: Soft bay coat still damp from birth, wide curious eyes. A single white star on the forehead.

subject: a spirited foal, already showing fire
details: Dark chestnut coat, ears pricked forward, one front leg pawing the dust. The spark is there.

subject: a foal with an old soul
details: Blue roan coat catching the light, head carried high on a long neck. Unusually composed for its age.

subject: a yearling filly, gangly but graceful
details: Pale palomino coat, flaxen mane catching the wind. Quiet confidence in her stance.

subject: a yearling colt, coiled energy
details: Seal brown coat, black points sharp against the gold light. Eyes bright with challenge.

subject: a yearling of obvious quality
details: Dappled gray already showing the pattern, elegant neck arch. The kind that stops conversations.

subject: a 2-year-old prospect, steady in the round pen
details: Solid sorrel coat, white blaze down the face. Moves with purpose, no wasted motion.

subject: a 2-year-old prospect, electric in the cut
details: Black coat with a single white sock. Explosive stop, ears flat, wholly focused.

subject: a 2-year-old prospect, head high
details: Buckskin with dorsal stripe, primitive markings. Carries itself like a finished horse.

subject: a seasoned campaigner, 6 years old
details: Bay roan, scar on the left shoulder from a past run. Unshakable in the box.

subject: a campaigner at the peak of power
details: Gristed black, muscles coiled. The turn is violence and grace in equal measure.

subject: a champion campaigner
details: Deep mahogany bay, white stockings on all four. Ribbons won, but the fire remains.

subject: an elder retiree, 18 years old
details: White-faced sorrel, sway back, gentle eyes. Has earned every gray hair.

subject: a retiree who remembers the work
details: Fleabitten gray, still moves with purpose when the saddle appears. The mind is sharp.

subject: a founding matriarch
details: Blue roan mare, broad hip, deep heart girth. Her blood runs through half the herd.
